message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker — close WASM compile closure epic and child tasks

Mark the WASM compile closure epic (asupersync-3qv04.1) and its child tasks
(1.1 through 1.6) as closed, reflecting the cfg-gating work completed in
the preceding commits. The parent Browser Edition epic (3qv04) remains open
with updated plan-space comments covering QA/forensics and DX tracks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3610,15 +3610,15 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-3qsbd","title":"Unit Tests: Lab Meta-Testing (mutation + runner, 804 LOC)","description":"Add inline unit tests for src/lab/meta/mutation.rs (440 LOC) and src/lab/meta/runner.rs (364 LOC). Mutation testing and meta-test orchestration. Tests: mutation operator application, mutant detection, runner scheduling, result aggregation, timeout handling, deterministic replay.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:13:26.986340685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T21:04:33.708006238Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T21:04:33.707919096Z","close_reason":"done","external_ref":"bd-2k1q","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lab","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qsbd","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1dm01","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1368,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qsbd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS: (1) Place tests in inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests {} block at bottom of the source file; (2) Each test must be self-contained — no external state, no network, no filesystem; (3) Test both success paths AND error paths; (4) Test boundary conditions (empty input, max values, zero-length, None variants); (5) Use descriptive test names: test_<what>_<condition>_<expected> pattern; (6) If the module has unsafe code or platform gates, test those paths with appropriate cfg attributes; (7) Target 80%+ line coverage of the module's public API.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:30:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qt18","title":"Sub-Epic: Runtime Debug Console - Ops-Grade Observability Tooling","description":"# Sub-Epic: Runtime Debug Console

## Overview
Implements a runtime debug console (TUI and/or web) providing ops-grade
observability for Asupersync applications, superior to tokio-console.

## Background & Motivation
Tokio has tokio-console with tracing ecosystem maturity. To win that comparison,
we need a cohesive tooling suite that surfaces:
- Region tree, tasks, cancellation state machine phase
- Obligations held, budgets remaining, stall reasons
- Deterministic trace capture and replay hooks
- 'Explain why' diagnostics

## Raid Plan: rich_rust Parts

### Available Parts
| Module | What to Raid | Target in asupersync |
|--------|--------------|---------------------|
| console.rs | Terminal detection, color downgrade | src/cli/console.rs |
| progress.rs | Progress bars for lab runtime | src/lab/progress.rs |
| table.rs | Table rendering for traces | src/trace/render.rs |
| tree.rs | Tree rendering for regions | src/cx/debug.rs |
| traceback.rs | Stack frame rendering | src/error/display.rs |

## Features

### 1. Runtime TUI
- Real-time region tree view
- Task list with status, state, budget
- Cancellation state machine visualization
- Obligation tracker
- Memory/CPU metrics

### 2. Web Dashboard (Optional)
- Browser-based interface
- WebSocket updates
- Trace timeline view
- Flame graphs

### 3. 'Explain Why' Diagnostics
- Why can't this region close?
- What's blocking this task?
- Why was this task cancelled?
- Which obligation is leaking?

### 4. Trace Capture
- Structured event recording
- Deterministic replay preparation
- Export to various formats

## Tasks in This Sub-Epic
1. Console rendering primitives (raid rich_rust)
2. Region tree visualization
3. Task inspector
4. Obligation tracker
5. Budget monitor
6. 'Explain why' diagnostics engine
7. Debug console E2E tests

## Integration Points
- Hooks into RuntimeState
- Trace buffer access
- Cx inspection
- Lab runtime integration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TUI shows live runtime state
- [ ] Region tree visualization works
- [ ] Task details accessible
- [ ] Obligations tracked and displayed
- [ ] 'Explain why' provides useful diagnostics
- [ ] Web dashboard (optional)
- [ ] Beautiful output (raid rich_rust)
- [ ] Low overhead when disabled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:33:50.003574882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:29:16.793908188Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:29:16.793805146Z","close_reason":"All children completed","external_ref":"bd-vpkn","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qt18","depends_on_id":"asupersync-33qcn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04","title":"[WASM] Ship Browser Edition as a usable JS/TS library","description":"Turn the current WASM/browser work from a contract-heavy preview into a real, installable, supportable JS/TS product lane.

Current reality this epic must resolve:
- the repo has wasm/browser profiles and strong guardrails, but Cargo explicitly says that preview gating does not imply full wasm parity;
- there is no actual packages/ tree, no package.json manifests, no pkg/ output directory, and no committed JS/TS package artifacts;
- there are no concrete #[wasm_bindgen] exports in src/ today, so the ABI/types are specified semantically but not yet surfaced as a consumable JS boundary;
- the browser reactor is still a scaffold/stub, not a working browser event-loop integration;
- the wasm browser-minimal Rust build is currently red due to native-surface leakage (config/http1 imports, reactor source export assumptions, socket2/libc usage, etc.).

Target product shape:
- direct runtime execution is for client-hydrated browser boundaries;
- server/node/edge contexts are bridge-only by design, not direct runtime lanes;
- ship real @asupersync/browser-core, @asupersync/browser, @asupersync/react, and @asupersync/next package surfaces;
- package artifacts, release gates, and onboarding must be backed by real build outputs and real consumer app validation rather than documentation-only or policy-only evidence.

This epic exists so future work can proceed entirely from Beads without consulting the original markdown planning material.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":2880,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:14:40.841937362Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:18:31.748088632Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bindings","browser","javascript","packaging","typescript","wasm"],"comments":[{"id":2393,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04","author":"CopperForest","text":"[2026-03-06 CopperForest] Baseline for this backlog: As of 2026-03-06, Asupersync is not yet a usable WASM/JS/TS library. The repo has substantial WASM/browser contracts, docs, and policy/test scaffolding, but not the actual product surface. Key facts this epic encodes so future work does not need the original markdown analysis: (1) Cargo explicitly frames wasm-browser-preview as guarded compilation, not full parity; (2) there is no packages/ tree, no committed package.json manifests, and no pkg/ output directory; (3) there are no concrete #[wasm_bindgen] exports in src/; (4) the browser reactor is still a scaffold/stub; (5) direct runtime execution is only a client-hydrated target, while server/node/edge are bridge-only by design; (6) remote check cargo check -p asupersync --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-default-features --features wasm-browser-minimal currently fails due native-surface leakage, including config/http1 imports, reactor/source assumptions, socket2 usage in net/tcp, and libc use in trace/file. Success for this epic means we have real packages, real bindings, real browser host integration, real consumer validation, and release gates backed by actual shipped artifacts rather than doc-only evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:34Z"},{"id":2401,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04","author":"CopperForest","text":"Current repo-state summary that justifies this epic:

- As of 2026-03-06, asupersync is not yet a usable JS/TS library in the practical sense of `install from npm`, import typed entrypoints, and build a browser app against real shipped artifacts.
- The repo already has substantial design/policy material for Browser Edition, but the implementation is still incomplete in the places that determine whether users can actually consume it.
- The intended direct-runtime target is the hydrated browser client boundary. Node/server/edge are bridge-only by design and should not silently drift into a fake "full runtime everywhere" story.

Concrete evidence captured during recon:

- There is no physical JS package tree today: no `packages/`, no `pkg/`, no `package.json` in the repo root or package subtrees.
- There are currently no concrete `wasm_bindgen` exports in `src/`; searches for `#[wasm_bindgen]`, `use wasm_bindgen`, `serde_wasm_bindgen`, and `tsify` came back empty.
- `src/runtime/reactor/browser.rs` is a scaffold/stub, not a finished host integration.
- `src/io/browser_stream.rs` documents that the wasm32 path "would interface" with JS host streams, which means the bridge is still aspirational instead of implemented.
- Several wasm tests currently validate docs/contracts and release-policy tokens, not installable package artifacts.

Operational goal of this epic:

Ship Browser Edition as something a real consumer can build against: compile-clean wasm profiles, real exported ABI, real package manifests/artifacts, framework adapters, and artifact-backed validation/release gates. The child tracks below decompose that goal so that future work does not need to rediscover either the current blockers or the intended product boundary.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:21:28Z"},{"id":2408,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04","author":"CopperForest","text":"Plan-space revision after graph audit: the first version captured the core implementation lanes, but it under-modeled three things that matter to users and to future execution quality.

1. Quality and failure-forensics needed to be explicit first-class work, not implied. A usable WASM/JS/TS library needs comprehensive unit/integration coverage, packaged E2E harnesses, rich logging, and retained artifacts so failures are debuggable.
2. Developer experience needed its own track. Examples, quickstarts, compatibility boundaries, troubleshooting, and reference/versioning guidance are part of product usability, not optional garnish.
3. The priority graph needed rebalancing so `bv` would recommend actionable dependency-clearing work instead of blocked integration milestones.

This revision therefore adds track `.8` for QA/forensics, track `.9` for DX/examples/docs, task `.5.7` for unsupported-runtime diagnostics, task `.6.7` for performance budgets, and lowers blocked integration epics/tasks so the compile-closure blockers surface first.","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:18:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","title":"[WASM] Rust wasm compile closure for canonical browser profiles","description":"Close the Rust-side wasm compilation gap first.

Why this is first:
- until wasm-browser-minimal compiles, nothing above it is trustworthy;
- the current failure is not cosmetic: native-only modules are still leaking into the wasm closure;
- getting minimal green establishes the true portability boundary and exposes the next real blockers instead of letting docs and policy drift ahead of implementation.

This track owns cfg-gating, platform-splitting, and compile-matrix restoration for the canonical wasm browser profiles.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":960,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:07.902051236Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:15:57.073438908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","cfg","compile","rust","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:07.902051236Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2394,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","author":"CopperForest","text":"[2026-03-06 CopperForest] This track is intentionally first because the current wasm-browser-minimal build is red. Until minimal compiles, the higher-level package and framework work would be built on an imaginary portability boundary. The current known leak set includes: config.rs importing h1 listener/server types that are cfg'd out on wasm, runtime/reactor/source.rs assuming only unix/windows platforms, net/tcp modules pulling socket2 on wasm, and trace/file.rs importing libc::ENOSPC. Restoring minimal first, then the broader dev/prod/deterministic compile matrix, is the foundation for every later track.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:34Z"}]}

This track owns cfg-gating, platform-splitting, and compile-matrix restoration for the canonical wasm browser profiles.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":960,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:07.902051236Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-07T03:26:31.888486403Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T03:26:31.888461126Z","close_reason":"All 6 child beads (1.1-1.6) are closed. WASM compile closure is complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","cfg","compile","rust","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:07.902051236Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2394,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","author":"CopperForest","text":"[2026-03-06 CopperForest] This track is intentionally first because the current wasm-browser-minimal build is red. Until minimal compiles, the higher-level package and framework work would be built on an imaginary portability boundary. The current known leak set includes: config.rs importing h1 listener/server types that are cfg'd out on wasm, runtime/reactor/source.rs assuming only unix/windows platforms, net/tcp modules pulling socket2 on wasm, and trace/file.rs importing libc::ENOSPC. Restoring minimal first, then the broader dev/prod/deterministic compile matrix, is the foundation for every later track.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.1","title":"[WASM][Compile] Gate native HTTP/server config imports out of wasm closure","description":"Split portable configuration from native-only HTTP listener/server configuration so wasm profiles do not pull cfg-gated h1 server types through src/config.rs. This task should leave future maintainers with a clean rule: portable config types stay in the wasm closure; host-listener/server config stays behind native cfg gates or a dedicated native config module.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.106772101Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:23:33.964369375Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:23:33.964346653Z","close_reason":"Gated native HTTP/server config out of wasm closure; wasm minimal check now advances to next TCP/socket blocker","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","config","http","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.106772101Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.2","title":"[WASM][Compile] Add wasm-safe reactor source platform/export path","description":"Add an explicit wasm-safe platform path for runtime/reactor/source.rs (or otherwise restructure the export surface) so reactor::Source and related types do not assume only unix/windows targets. This task closes one of the current build failures and defines the portability model for registerable sources in browser builds.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.148189448Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:35:07.556440128Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:35:07.556414460Z","close_reason":"Current tree adds a wasm-safe reactor source platform branch; canonical remote wasm-browser-minimal cargo check is green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","reactor","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.148189448Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.3","title":"[WASM][Compile] Isolate native TCP/socket surfaces from browser profiles","description":"Remove native TCP/socket machinery from the wasm browser closure. The current wasm build still reaches socket2-backed tcp modules, which is a hard leak of native networking assumptions into browser builds. This task should establish a clear browser-side alternative boundary or cfg split instead of papering over imports.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.190997969Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:49:21.182695108Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:49:21.182671384Z","close_reason":"Completed: isolated native TCP/socket/async poll paths behind explicit wasm Unsupported boundaries; canonical rch wasm-browser-minimal cargo check is green; remaining clippy failures are unrelated runtime/kernel.rs lint debt","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","net","socket","tcp","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.190997969Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.4","title":"[WASM][Compile] Isolate native trace/file paths from wasm closure","description":"Remove libc/file-trace native assumptions from the wasm closure, including ENOSPC handling in trace/file paths. The point is not just to silence one import; it is to decide which trace/file surfaces are native-only and which browser-compatible trace sinks remain available in wasm profiles.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.233500202Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:38:01.238383219Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:38:01.238360357Z","close_reason":"Wasm trace/file compile blocker is resolved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","file","trace","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.233500202Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","title":"[WASM][Compile] Make wasm-browser-minimal compile green","description":"Drive the current remote wasm-browser-minimal cargo check to green after the native-surface leaks are fixed. This is the first hard acceptance point for the entire backlog: if minimal does not compile, there is still no trustworthy foundation for packages or adapters.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.275813904Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:40:55.979216475Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:40:55.979193462Z","close_reason":"Canonical remote minimal gate passed: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-wasm-min cargo check -p asupersync --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-default-features --features wasm-browser-minimal","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","minimal","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.275813904Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:03.942872031Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:03.985929685Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:04.026700361Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:04.073360988Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2402,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","author":"CopperForest","text":"Repro and current acceptance baseline:

Run this exact remote compile as the canonical minimal-gate smoke test:
`rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-wasm-min cargo check -p asupersync --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-default-features --features wasm-browser-minimal`

This failed remotely during recon with 19 errors, which is why compile closure is the first hard workstream instead of package polish. Representative blockers observed at the time:
- `src/config.rs:11-12`: native HTTP listener/server config imports leaking into the wasm closure
- `src/runtime/reactor/source.rs:267`: platform export path missing a wasm branch
- `src/net/tcp/stream.rs:15` and `src/net/tcp/socket.rs:127+`: native `socket2` usage not isolated from browser profiles
- `src/trace/file.rs:64`: native `libc::ENOSPC` path leaking into wasm

Definition-of-done for this bead is not "some wasm code compiles" but "the canonical remote minimal profile check above goes green without local fail-open fallback".","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:23:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.6","title":"[WASM][Compile] Restore dev/prod/deterministic wasm profile matrix","description":"Expand from minimal to the full canonical profile matrix: wasm-browser-dev, wasm-browser-prod, and wasm-browser-deterministic. This task should capture the first-order differences between the profiles and ensure browser-io/browser-trace additions do not reintroduce native leakage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.317466067Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:50.980907247Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:50.980881389Z","close_reason":"Restored wasm profile matrix: wasm-browser-dev/prod/deterministic all compile via rch; src/io/read_line.rs borrow fix was required for browser-io profiles.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","profiles","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.317466067Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:04.116870582Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.2","title":"[WASM] Concrete binding/export boundary and wasm artifact generation","description":"Materialize the actual JS/WASM boundary.

Why this matters:
- the repo currently has ABI contracts and dispatcher semantics, but not concrete wasm-bindgen exports;
- without a real exported boundary, there is no consumable runtime_create/runtime_close/scope/task/fetch surface for JS or TS callers;
- artifact shape, d.ts generation, and ABI fingerprint embedding all depend on making the binding layer real.

This track owns the concrete export boundary, marshalling strategy, and generated artifact layout.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":960,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:07.950938122Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:15:57.073656072Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","artifact","bindings","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:07.950938122Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:03.546550010Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2395,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2","author":"CopperForest","text":"[2026-03-06 CopperForest] This track exists because the current ABI layer is mostly semantic/contractual. The repo defines WasmAbiVersion, symbol tables, payload shapes, compatibility policy, and dispatcher semantics, but there are no concrete #[wasm_bindgen] exports in src/ yet. Future implementers should treat this as the point where the planned JS boundary becomes real: exported symbols, typed marshalling, ABI fingerprint embedding, and concrete artifact generation.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.1","title":"[WASM][Bindings] Decide concrete wasm artifact strategy and crate layout","description":"Decide and record the concrete artifact strategy for the shipped wasm library surface. The current repo has a plain Rust lib target and semantic ABI contracts, but not a committed decision for whether the browser product should be produced directly from this crate, from a dedicated bindings crate, or from another artifact assembly path.  {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.2","title":"[WASM][Bindings] Implement concrete exported ABI surface for v1 symbols","description":"Implement the actual exported ABI surface for the documented v1 symbol table (runtime_create, runtime_close, scope/task/fetch operations). This task is where the planned JS boundary becomes real: concrete exports, concrete ownership/handle dispatch, and concrete compatibility checks rather than contract-only types.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.402548396Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:19:15.265158447Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","bindings","export","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:04.243987936Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.402548396Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:04.201299127Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2403,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.2","author":"CopperForest","text":"Why this bead exists:

The repo already contains ABI/versioning intent in `src/types/wasm_abi.rs`, but there is currently no evidence of a real exported JS-facing boundary in the implementation. Recon across `src/` found no uses of:
- `#[wasm_bindgen]`
- `use wasm_bindgen`
- `serde_wasm_bindgen`
- `tsify`

That means Browser Edition currently has a contract surface without an actual generated/exported surface. This bead is the conversion point from policy/spec to product: concrete exported functions, handle ownership, lifetime/error behavior, and version-compatibility checks.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:24:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.3","title":"[WASM][Bindings] Implement JS<->WASM payload and error marshalling","description":"Implement the payload, error, outcome, and cancellation marshalling strategy across the JS boundary. The goal is to preserve Asupersync semantics at the boundary, not to flatten everything into opaque strings or JS exceptions. This task should cover value encoding, compatibility rejection, cancellation fidelity, and structured diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.447359509Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:16:02.173886966Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bindings","errors","marshalling","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:16:20.447359509Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:04.287955090Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
